2606.24311.title: LemonHarness Technical Report
2606.24311.paper_summary: As large language model (LLM) agents are applied to longer tasks, they increasingly modify workspace state across multiple rounds of iteration. However, agents typically observe only tool outputs and log fragments, while the actual state changes occur in the file system. Without explicit workspace boundaries, state-changing operations such as file writes and temporary artifact generation may scatter changes across paths. Over time, these weakly constrained changes accumulate, making states such as modified files difficult to track. This paper presents LemonHarness, an integrated execution framework for long-horizon agents. LemonHarness establishes an explicit execution boundary by constraining state-changing operations within a clearly defined workspace and bringing model invocation, tool execution, and rule knowledge within a single controlled boundary. State-changing operations, including file writes, dependency installation, and temporary artifact creation, are executed through structured tool interfaces, with execution feedback recorded as observations available to subsequent model decisions. The system also introduces a reusable rule knowledge base, which turns recurring execution rules and acceptance criteria into runtime knowledge. LemonHarness further adds a time-aware execution mechanism that exposes elapsed and remaining budget to the model, so it can rebalance exploration, implementation, and validation effort as time pressure shifts and avoid timeouts from long waits or excessive verification. On Terminal-Bench 2.0, LemonHarness_GPT-5.3-CodeX reached 84.49% accuracy over 445 trials; pairing the same framework with the stronger GPT-5.5 backbone raised the average accuracy to 86.52% across five jobs. The results suggest that a unified runtime boundary, callable rule knowledge, and time-aware execution can improve the stability of long-horizon agent execution.
2606.24311.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLM）代理被應用於更長的任務，它們越來越多地在多輪迭代中修改工作區狀態。<lb/>然而，代理通常僅觀察工具輸出和日誌片段，而實際的狀態變更發生在文件系統中。<lb/>在沒有明確工作區邊界的情況下，像文件寫入和臨時工件生成這樣的狀態變更操作可能會將變更分散到不同的路徑中。<lb/>隨著時間的推移，這些弱約束的變更累積，使得像修改過的文件這樣的狀態難以追蹤。<lb/>本文介紹了LemonHarness，一個針對長期代理的集成執行框架。<lb/>LemonHarness通過將狀態變更操作限制在明確定義的工作區內，並將模型調用、工具執行和規則知識納入單一受控邊界，建立了一個明確的執行邊界。<lb/>狀態變更操作，包括文件寫入、依賴安裝和臨時工件創建，通過結構化的工具接口執行，執行反饋作為觀察記錄，供後續模型決策使用。<lb/>系統還引入了一個可重用的規則知識庫，將重複的執行規則和接受標準轉化為運行時知識。<lb/>LemonHarness進一步增加了一個時間感知的執行機制，將已過時間和剩餘預算暴露給模型，以便在時間壓力變化時重新平衡探索、實施和驗證的努力，並避免因長時間等待或過度驗證而導致的超時。<lb/>在Terminal-Bench 2.0上，LemonHarness_GPT-5.3-CodeX在445次試驗中達到了84.49%的準確率；將相同框架與更強的GPT-5.5骨幹配對，將五個任務的平均準確率提高到86.52%。<lb/>結果表明，統一的運行時邊界、可調用的規則知識和時間感知的執行可以提高長期代理執行的穩定性。
2606.24311.author: Kailong Ren et.al.
2606.24311.authors: Kailong Ren, Fubo Sun, Jiachen Liu, Liu Yang, Zimo Yin, Jiaying Li, Congli Yin, Ming He, Yu Huo, Jiawei Liu, Zeping Chen, Yubin Huangfu, Ronghua Li, Yixuan Wu, Xing Su, Yanzhi Xu, Likang Wu, Hongke Zhao, Lei Zhang, Xiaohui Geng, Jianping Fan
